item.title: Global first-quarter M&A exceeds $1.2 trillion, led by AI
item.description: Turbulence linked to the war in Iran and swings in valuations have yet to deter corporate deal-making as transactions in the first quarter exceeded a record of $1.2 trillion, LSEG data shows, and dealmakers ​say much more is in the pipeline.

item.title: Chinese chipmakers claim nearly half of local market as Nvidia's lead shrinks
item.description: Chinese GPU and AI chip ‌makers captured nearly 41% of China's AI accelerator server market last year, eroding Nvidia's once-dominant position in one of its most important overseas markets, according to data from an IDC ​report reviewed by Reuters.

item.title: PE firm Arcline walks away from bid for UK's Senior as other suitors circle
item.description: The field of suitors for British aerospace and ​defence supplier Senior Plc narrowed after private ‌equity firm Arcline Investment Management dropped out of buyout talks on Wednesday, though negotiations with other bidders remained ongoing.

item.title: New BP CEO takes helm pledging consistency, staff note shows
item.description: BP's new CEO Meg O'Neill told employees on Wednesday she would offer consistency while accelerating the group's performance, a year after BP pivoted its strategy firmly back to oil and ​gas, according to a staff note seen by Reuters.

item.title: Euro zone factory growth hits 45-month high amid supply disruptions, PMI shows
item.description: Euro zone manufacturing growth bounced to its strongest in nearly four years in March as supply chain disruptions inflated growth figures although underlying demand remained ​tepid and soaring input costs due to the Iran warthreatened to undermine the sector's ‌fragile recovery, a survey showed.

item.title: Microsoft on track to invest $5.5 billion in Singapore by 2029, WSJ reports
item.description: Microsoft is ​on track ‌to invest $5.5 billion ​in ​cloud and artificial ⁠intelligence ​infrastructure in ​Singapore through 2029, the ​Wall ​Street Journal reported ‌on ⁠Wednesday.

item.title: Tesla French car registrations triple in March
item.description: Tesla new car registrations in France more than tripled in March and were just shy of an all-time high recorded ​over two years ago, national data showed on ‌Wednesday in the latest sign of the EV giant's sales recovery in Europe.

item.title: China's CAS Space targets $607 million IPO for reusable rocket R&D
item.description: China's CAS Space Technology is seeking to raise around 4.18 billion yuan ($607 million) in a planned listing on ​Shanghai's tech-focussed STAR Market, according to a filing on Tuesday, ‌as Chinese space firms take advantage of government support to pursue initial public offerings.

item.title: Baidu robotaxi outage in Wuhan caused by 'system failure', police say
item.description: A "system failure" caused a robotaxi outage involving multiple vehicles operated by Baidu's Apollo Go in central China's Wuhan, local police ​said on Wednesday, re-igniting safety concerns over the fast-growing service.

item.title: Morning Bid: April fools rush in
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter:

item.title: China's new home prices rise in March; big cities see seasonal pickup, private survey shows
item.description: China's new home prices edged up in March, reversing a decline in the previous month, supported by a seasonal ​pickup in demand in major cities, a private survey ‌showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Qantas, American Airlines get interim Australia nod for trans-Pacific tie-up
item.description: Australia's competition ‌regulator said on Wednesday it has granted interim authorisation to Qantas ​and American Airlines ​to continue cooperating on ⁠trans-Pacific routes linking Australia and New ​Zealand with U.S., Canada and ​Mexico.

item.title: TSMC plans 3-nanometre chip production launch in Japan in 2028
item.description: TSMC , the world's largest contract chipmaker, is expected to launch equipment installation and ​mass production of 3-nanometre wafers in 2028 at ‌its second factory in Japan, according to a Taiwanese government filing late on Tuesday.

item.title: Asia's factory activity slows on cost pressure from Iran war
item.description: Many ​Asian economies saw factory activity slow in March, business surveys showed on Wednesday, a ‌sign surging fuel costs and heightening global uncertainty from the Iran war were taking a toll on the region.

item.title: Asia markets rally on optimism Iran war could end soon
item.description: Stocks and bonds rallied and the dollar wallowed at the ‌start of the Asian trading session on Wednesday on hopes of a de-escalation in the Iran conflict, while significantly better-than-expected economic data for March propelled a rebound in Korean and Japanese shares.

item.title: Sequoia Capital names veteran Doug Leone as chairman
item.description: Sequoia Capital has named former senior steward Doug Leone as chairman, ​partners at the storied Silicon Valley venture capital firm ‌said in X posts on Tuesday.

item.title: Dollar stays stable after Trump says Iran war could finish soon
item.description: The dollar was largely steady ​on Wednesday, with gains from safe-haven demand fading, as U.S. President Donald Trump said the war with ‌Iran could wind down within weeks, though conflicting signals on the conflict kept investors on edge.

item.title: Front-month Brent oil futures extend gains after record monthly rise in March
item.description: Oil prices ticked up in early Wednesday trade, with Brent front-month futures extending a record March rally as Middle East volatility kept markets jittery, despite reports that the ​U.S. and Iran may be edging closer to a negotiated end to the war.

item.title: Apple's 50-year journey from garage to tech titan
item.description: In early 1976 in California, Steve Wozniak had just completed the design of a computer circuit board he intended to share with fellow hobbyists at a prominent local club. His friend Steve Jobs also saw a business opportunity ​to manufacture and sell the boards, and thus Apple was born.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lines up 21 banks for mega IPO, code-named project Apex
item.description: SpaceX is working with at least 21 banks on its blockbuster initial public offering, people familiar ​with the matter said on Tuesday, one of the largest underwriting ‌syndicates assembled in recent years.

item.title: New Zealand's Fonterra admits 'grass-fed' label may mislead shoppers after Greenpeace lawsuit
item.description: New Zealand's Fonterra said on Wednesday it had settled a lawsuit filed by environmental group Greenpeace by ​admitting the label on one of its products ‌was likely to mislead some shoppers about what its cows were actually fed.

item.title: Microsoft, Chevron and Engine No. 1 sign exclusive deal for power supply
item.description: Microsoft , Chevron and investment fund Engine No. 1 have entered into an exclusivity agreement for power ​generation and supply, the three companies said on Tuesday.

item.title: Insider trading in prediction markets is among CFTC priorities, top cop says
item.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission will focus on policing market ​misconduct such as insider trading in prediction markets and market ‌manipulation in energy markets, the agency's new enforcement director said on Tuesday.

item.title: Republican senator proposes to widen US ban on Chinese autos
item.description: Republican Senator Bernie Moreno said on Tuesday he will introduce legislation next month to toughen a U.S. government ban on Chinese automakers from entering the American market, and encouraged other countries to ​follow suit.

item.title: Anthropic to sign deal with Australia on AI safety and economic data tracking
item.description: Anthropic said on Wednesday it would sign an agreement to share its economic index data ​with the Australian government to help track ‌artificial intelligence adoption across the economy, and its impact on workers and jobs.

item.title: AT&T signs deal worth $2 billion to upgrade emergency cellular network
item.description: AT&T has reached a deal to invest about $1 billion to ​improve the Commerce Department's FirstNet ‌and deliver $1 billion in cost savings for the program via reduced rates, a U.S ​government agency said on Tuesday.

item.title: Exclusive: Investor Artisan Partners backs Unilever's plan to sell food unit
item.description: Artisan Partners, ​which has a history of driving strategic change at consumer companies, welcomed Unilever's decision on Tuesday to merge its ‌food business with McCormick , saying the Dove soap maker should now be able to "more effectively manage" its core personal care and home brands.

item.title: X down for thousands of users in the US, Downdetector shows
item.description: Social media platform ​X was down ‌for thousands of users ​in ​the U.S. on Tuesday, ⁠according ​to Downdetector.com.

item.title: Trading Day: Q1 ends with a BANG!
item.description: Wall Street closed a tumultuous first quarter with a bang on Tuesday as hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East powered stocks to their best day in almost a year, even as data showed a slump in U.S. job openings and hirings.

item.title: Beyond Meat misses quarterly results estimates on persistent demand struggles
item.description: Faux meat maker Beyond Meat missed Wall Street estimates for fourth-quarter sales and posted a larger-than-expected operating loss on Tuesday due ​to sluggish demand for its higher-priced processed food products.
